name: ATOSSA THERAPEUTICS, INC.
business: Atossa Therapeutics is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing proprietary medicines in oncology, with a focus on breast cancer and other breast conditions. Its lead drug candidate is oral (Z)-endoxifen, a selective estrogen receptor modulator/degrader currently in Phase 2 clinical development. The company is not yet marketing any products and does not anticipate revenue until its programs are developed and approved.

exposures.sentence: We are a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing proprietary innovative medicines in areas of significant unmet medical need in oncology, with a focus on breast cancer and other breast conditions.
Our lead drug candidate is oral (Z)-endoxifen, a selective estrogen receptor modulator (SERM)/ selective estrogen receptor degrader (SERM/D) currently in Phase 2 clinical development.

Based on its mechanism of action, we are exploring the potential application of (Z)-endoxifen beyond breast cancer, including gynecological cancers, endocrine resistance driven by ESR1 mutations, as well as applications in other rare disease indications, including Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy (DMD), women carriers of DMD, and McCune-Albright Syndrome (MAS) in girls.
We have been granted U.S. and international patents covering our proprietary (Z)-endoxifen, and we have numerous applications pending in the U.S. and in other major countries.

catalysts.event: Conclusion of 24-month mammographic record review for Karisma-(Z)-endoxifen study
catalysts.sentence: The follow up 24-month mammographic record review of participants is expected to conclude by the end of the first quarter in 2026.

catalysts.event: Receipt of durability data for Karisma-(Z)-endoxifen study
catalysts.sentence: Durability data is expected to be received at the end of Q2.

catalysts.event: Top-line data report from Karisma-(Z)-endoxifen study
catalysts.sentence: We expect to report top-line data from the Karisma-(Z)-endoxifen study in the first half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Begin receiving data from expanded arms of I-SPY 2 study (postmenopausal combination arm)
catalysts.sentence: Enrollment for the two primary arms of this expanded study using (Z)-endoxifen as a combination therapy is nearly complete and we expect to begin receiving data early in the second half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Begin receiving data from premenopausal arms of I-SPY 2 study
catalysts.sentence: Enrollment is nearly complete for these arms, and we expect to begin receiving data in the second half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Pursuit of Orphan Drug Designation for women carriers of DMD
catalysts.sentence: Additionally, we believe this condition meets the requirements of and could qualify for Orphan Drug Designation, and we intend to pursue this designation in the first half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Pursuit of Rare Pediatric Disease and Orphan Drug designations for MAS
catalysts.sentence: Given the age of impacted girls and the relatively small size of this impacted population, we expect to seek both Rare Pediatric Disease and Orphan Drug designations for MAS in the first half of 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Global ER+ breast cancer treatment market anticipated to reach ~$30 billion by 2030
tamClaims.sentence: According to a 2025 report by Strategic Market Research, the global ER+ breast cancer treatment market is anticipated to reach approximately $30 billion by 2030 and is projected to grow at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of approximately 7% through 2030.

tamClaims.claim: Potential U.S. market for (Z)-endoxifen in breast cancer could be up to $1 billion or more annually
tamClaims.sentence: We believe that, based in part on a study by Defined Health Inc. (now Lumanity), a leading market research firm, the potential U.S. market for (Z)-endoxifen in each indication in breast cancer treatment and prevention settings could be up to $1 billion or more, annually.

tamClaims.claim: Global DMD treatment market estimated ~$3 billion in 2025, projected to grow to ~$10 billion by 2030
tamClaims.sentence: The global therapeutics market for DMD treatment is estimated to be approximately $3 billion in 2025, and is projected to grow to potentially $10 billion by 2030.

tamClaims.claim: MAS treatment market estimated at as much as $1 billion annually within broader rare bone and endocrinology markets
tamClaims.sentence: Treatment options for MAS are limited, with significant unmet medical need, and are estimated at as much as $1 billion annually within broader rare bone and endocrinology therapeutic markets.
